text: Green hydrogen, produced from non-carbonaceous sources using carbon-neutral processes, will be a key element in the realization of the ambitious reductions in emissions necessary to honor the Paris Agreement. Amongst all technologies, proton exchange membrane water electrolyzers (PEMWEs) are currently leading the charge due to their high efficiency and fast transient response. The PEMWE production capacity is currently still below one gigawatt globally. In order to enable the production of the required amount of hydrogen in line with the needed climate change mitigation measures, the global capacity has to be enhanced to hundreds of gigawatts by 2030. This paper is intended to act as a seminal reference for necessary technological advancements needed to reach the required performance at the cell level, including commentary from executives of three leading industrial hydrogen production companies. The necessary considerations for each component, as well as the multidisciplinary analysis on economies of scale, renewable energy costs, and policy development are included. In addition, the necessary fundamental scientific advancements for the improvement of component performance are outlined and the recommended corresponding accelerated stress tests (ASTs) are discussed to ensure the longevity of the components at the required production scale.
headline: Fully Human Written
